fix(branches): scope members tab authorization to specific branch

The Members tab on Branch view had two security issues:
- Members were eagerly loaded via contain() regardless of permissions
- Authorization check used global table-level canIndex instead of
  branch-scoped check

Now creates a probe Member entity with the branch ID and checks
canIndex against it, so BasePolicy._hasPolicy properly validates
the user permission for that specific branch. Members data is
only loaded when authorized.

Applies to both view() and edit() actions.

commit.message:
fix: parallel fan-out/fan-in + labeled predecessor outputs

Problems fixed:
1. resolveNextNode only returned first edge target — fan-out to parallel
   branches (e.g. 毛泽东+马斯克) was silently dropped
2. Fan-in (join) missing — 巴菲特 ran without waiting for both predecessors
3. availableInputs flat-merged all outputs (key collisions, lost source info)
4. ctx.messages truncated to last 3 — downstream nodes got incomplete history

Fixes:
- Replace resolveNextNode with resolveNextNodes returning all valid targets
- Add getNodePredecessors + isNodeReady helpers for join detection
- Add pendingParallelNodes: string[] to ExecutionContext
- completeNode: fan-out → queue extra targets; fan-in → check all preds done
  before advancing; pop pendingParallelNodes when join not yet ready
- availableInputs now { globalContext, predecessorOutputs: { nodeLabel: output } }
  so each node clearly sees which output came from which predecessor
- Remove messages.slice(-3) — pass full message history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add Foundry evaluator-optimizer integration (experimental) (#384)

## Summary

Adds Azure Foundry agent evaluator integration for LangGraph,
implementing the [evaluator-optimizer
pattern](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/workflows-agents#evaluator-optimizer)
with real [Foundry builtin
evaluators](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/concepts/evaluation-evaluators/agent-evaluators).
All new APIs are marked `@experimental()`.

## Library: `langchain_azure_ai.evaluation` (experimental)

New evaluation module with Foundry evaluator wrappers:

- **`FoundryEvaluator`** — wraps Foundry builtin evaluators, creates
eval definitions, runs them, polls for results, and parses outputs
- **`FoundryEvaluatorSuite`** — runs multiple evaluators against the
same input, tracks last results via `all_passed`, and now exposes
`close()` to propagate cached-client cleanup
- **`messages_to_foundry_format()`** — converts LangChain messages to
Foundry/OpenAI-style query/response payloads
- **`tool_schemas_to_foundry_format()`** — converts LangChain tool
objects to Foundry tool definitions
- **`create_eval_optimize_subgraph()`** — reusable LangGraph builder for
evaluate → conditional refine loops
- **`create_analyst_subgraph()`** — analyst-oriented helper now
parameterized via transformation callbacks instead of sample-specific
imports/state assumptions

## Tracer integration

- Adds `gen_ai.evaluation.*` semantic convention constants to the tracer
`Attrs` surface
- Adds `emit_evaluation_event()` to `AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer` for
`gen_ai.evaluation.result` events attached to the evaluated
`invoke_agent` span

## Review-driven refinements

- Removed sample-specific `importlib.import_module("eval_config")`
coupling from library code
- Enforced iteration limits in graph state rather than a shared closure
counter, making eval-optimize loops per-invocation and concurrency-safe
- Added reusable `state_to_eval_input` / `eval_output_to_state`
callbacks so analyst helpers can adapt caller-specific state schemas
- Hardened section/state access so dict/object-shaped inputs are handled
safely
- Cached/reused Foundry clients with locking and added `close()`
lifecycle support
- `FoundryEvaluatorSuite.close()` now propagates evaluator cleanup so
suite users can release cached project/OpenAI clients
- `_get_client()` now takes the client lock even when returning cached
clients, avoiding races with `close()`, and its implementation/docstring
were simplified to match the actual control flow
- Switched polling to `time.monotonic()` for accurate timeout tracking
- Timed-out eval runs now return explicit failed `FoundryEvalResult`
values with timeout explanations
- Normalized label -> passed handling consistently across response
parsing branches
- Removed duplicate unused evaluation constants from the legacy
`_semantic_conventions_gen_ai.py` module so the tracer has a single
canonical attribute definition surface

## Tests

Expanded unit coverage now validates:
- Message/tool conversion helpers
- Foundry evaluator wrappers and timeout behavior
- Client reuse behavior and locked cached-client reads
- Suite-level cleanup via `FoundryEvaluatorSuite.close()`
- Consistent result parsing across API shapes
- Tracer `emit_evaluation_event()` output
- Evaluator-optimizer helpers, concurrent iteration limiting, custom
state mapping, and safe section handling

Targeted validation currently passes (`test_evaluation.py`, ruff, mypy
on changed files).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor get_storage_path* functions (#12906)

We were using Project.get_storage_path, which required to manually pass
attributes from a version. Most of the time we already have a version
object in place, so I "moved" the method to the Version class. The old
method was also used to get file of a downloadable media type, I moved
this to another method. Version.get_storage_path also accepts a
filename, so we don't need to join the paths manually everytime we want
to get the storage path from a file.

We also were setting a version_type attribute to force all storage calls
to be external/internal, but we should make sure the version we are
getting the file from matches the external domain it's served from, this
check is already done by the unresolver. We also have tests for this
already.

Project.get_storage_paths wasn't returning the paths used by PR
previews, we were missing deleting those from storage when the project
was deleted.

Ref https://github.com/readthedocs/readthedocs.org/issues/12899
Closes https://github.com/readthedocs/readthedocs.org/issues/10220

fix: suppress interactive apt/needrestart prompts during provisioning

cloud-init runs without a TTY, so any interactive dialog (needrestart,
debconf whiptail) fails with 'Failed to open terminal' and pollutes the
provision log with ANSI/VT100 escape sequences.

Add to all provisioning scripts (bootstrap.sh, setup.sh, proxy.sh,
agents.sh) and the configurator generator template:

  export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  export NEEDRESTART_MODE=a

DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive prevents debconf from launching
interactive frontends. NEEDRESTART_MODE=a makes needrestart
automatically restart services without prompting.

commit.message:
fix(proxy): re-apply chown after caddy validate to fix log permission

caddy validate runs as root and can create /var/log/caddy/access.log
owned by root before Caddy's systemd service (running as caddy user)
gets a chance to write to it.

Moving the chown to after validate (and before systemctl restart)
ensures the caddy user always owns the log file at startup.

Fixes recurring ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED on fresh provisioning.

commit.message:
Fix camera depth supervision: reference-based approach

Replace Gaussian-rendered depth (corrupted by 547m-scale Gaussians)
with a pre-computed GT-projected reference depth image.

Changes:
- Add _build_cam_depth_refs(): projects LiDAR pts at GT T_l2c,
  scatter-min rasterises to sparse depth image (H, W), one per frame
- Pre-compute all reference images before training loop when
  lambda_cam_depth > 0
- Replace render_camera_raytracing() block with reference lookup:
  bilinear-sample d_ref at projected pixel coords; gradient flows
  only through d_lidar(T_l2c) → T_l2c, not through Gaussians
- Update viz_cam_depth_supervision.py to use the same reference
  approach: Panel 2 now shows GT-projected reference depth instead
  of Gaussian-rendered depth; remove gaussians dependency from
  visualise_frame()

Root cause addressed:
  LiDAR-trained Gaussians have max scale=547m; these huge Gaussians
  act as infinite planes blocking all camera rays (~26m depth instead
  of expected ~15m). The new approach bypasses Gaussian rendering
  entirely for the camera depth supervision reference.

Also removes render_camera_raytracing import from viz script.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix private method access on proxy objects: bypass proxy traps for private keys in GetMethod

When a class constructor returns a Proxy wrapping 'this', private fields
and methods are installed on the proxy object (not the target). GetMethod
was calling read_named_property which triggers the proxy's get trap,
causing the trap to look for private keys on the target (which doesn't
have them). Per spec, private name resolution should never go through
[[Get]]/proxy traps. Now GetMethod reads directly from the object's map
for private keys, matching GetProperty's existing behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add runtime ZSign integration for JITLess code signing

The loader now automatically signs downloaded payloads using
LiveContainer's bundled ZSign.dylib:

1. Direct dlopen (works if JIT enabled)
2. Ad-hoc sign via ZSigner.adhocSignMachOAtPath + retry
3. Full sign with LC's P12 cert from NSUserDefaults + retry

Each step logs detailed progress. Overlay shows which signing
approach is being attempted. Falls back gracefully if ZSign
isn't available.

fix: resolve provisioning failures in cloud-init environment

Three bugs caused complete provisioning failure when run via cloud-init:

1. HOME not set: cloud-init runcmd runs without a login shell, so the
   HOME env var is unset. The Coder install script requires HOME to
   resolve cache/install paths.
   Fix: export HOME=\ in bootstrap.sh and setup.sh.

2. Wrong Coder binary path: setup.sh hardcoded ExecStart=/usr/bin/coder
   but Coder installs to /usr/local/bin/coder (--method=standalone).
   Fix: use /usr/local/bin/coder in the systemd unit.

3. Coder running as root: Coder's built-in PostgreSQL refuses to start
   as root. The systemd unit had no User= directive, defaulting to root.
   Fix: create a dedicated 'coder' system user and set User=coder/Group=coder.
   Also switched deprecated CODER_ADDRESS to CODER_HTTP_ADDRESS.

4. Caddy log permission: proxy.sh created /var/log/caddy as root, then
   Caddy (running as caddy user) couldn't write access.log.
   Fix: chown -R caddy:caddy /var/log/caddy after mkdir.

Closes #15

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: sink mute detection, recovery guidance, and auto-unmute safety net (#123)

Four-layer defense against system-level PA sink mute going unnoticed:

1. UI warning: device card shows orange 'Sink muted' status with blocked
   hint and one-click 'Unmute speaker' action when PA sink is muted but
   app-level mute is off

2. Health degradation: compute_device_health_state() reports degraded
   state with sink_muted_at_system_level reason; new SINK_MUTED and
   SINK_UNMUTED device events in status_event_builder

3. Recovery guidance: new sink_system_muted issue in operator recovery
   assistant with 'Unmute speaker' primary action; new /api/unmute_sink
   endpoint for direct PA sink unmute bypassing MA routing

4. Auto-unmute safety net: parent-side watchdog auto-unmutes after 30s
   grace period if desync persists; daemon startup unmute retries up to
   3x on failure with 2s intervals

commit.message:
Use CodeRangeMapRangeList for callcounting stubs and remove unneeded StubManagers (#126521)

In order to figure out if a specific address is a callcounting stub, we
currently have to iterate through the call counting stub allocators to
figure out which one, if any, owns the address. If we switch call
counting stubs to use CodeRangeMapRangeList, the type of the code can
easily be looked up from the cDAC in the RangeSectionMap.

Removed EnumMem of the call counting stub heap. We now have the same
enumeration as other CodeRangeMapRangeList users. That is, the
RangeSection metadata allows lookup of the code type etc, but the actual
bytes of the stubs are not explicitly included in a heap dump.

Relevant for
https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/1d27484034940eee7eb09dec62c5138125346802/src/coreclr/debug/daccess/daccess.cpp#L5441

---------
